title: Warfarin drug-drug interactions: high-yield list
key_recommendations: Amiodarone potentiates warfarin via CYP2C9 and CYP3A4 inhibition. Expect INR to rise within 5-10 days; reduce warfarin dose by 30-50% pre-emptively or monitor INR every 3-4 days for 2 weeks.
Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole strongly potentiates warfarin via CYP2C9 inhibition.
Metronidazole, ciprofloxacin, and fluconazole all elevate INR.
NSAIDs do not affect INR directly but increase bleeding risk via platelet inhibition and GI mucosal injury.
Acetaminophen at doses >2 g/day chronically can modestly elevate INR.
Rifampin, carbamazepine, and St John's Wort lower INR via CYP induction.
